heading: Settlement or compromise: final and conclusive
label: 9806 Settlement or compromise: final and conclusive
text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, upon acceptance of payment the settlement or compromise of a claim under section 9802 or 9803 of this title is final and conclusive.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)980610:861 (35 words before 1st proviso).10:862 (last 32 words of 2d sentence)., §§ 1 (35 words before 1st proviso), 2 (last 32 words of 2d sentence), , 573.Oct. 20, 1951, ch. 52465 Stat. 572The words “for all purposes” and “to the contrary”, in 10:1861 and 1862; “by the claimant and not until then”, in 10:1861; and “but not until then”, in 10:1862; are omitted as surplusage.
